text.title: AI Latency Waterfall Template Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every waterfall investigation enforces.

rule.statement: Latency is reported per segment (ttfb, prefill, decode_tps, per-tool round-trip, post-process). A single end-to-end number is not acceptable as a waterfall.

rationale.source: OpenTelemetry spans practice; LangSmith trace anatomy

rule.rationale: Aggregates hide the bottleneck; the entire methodology is the decomposition.

rule.statement: Each segment carries a budget. The report flags every segment whose observed p95 exceeds budget.

rationale.source: Google SRE workbook ch.4; latency SLOs

rule.rationale: Budgets force prioritisation: 50ms over budget on prefill matters; 10ms over budget on post-process usually does not.

rule.statement: Each segment value is computed over ≥30 samples drawn within the same observation window. Anything less is noise.
rule.rationale: Smaller samples produce wildly different percentiles; recommendations off such data are wrong half the time.

rule.statement: Each segment reports p50 AND p95 (or higher). p50 alone misses tail behaviour; p95 alone misses headline.

rationale.source: Google SRE workbook; latency distribution literature

rule.rationale: Long-tail latency in AI is dominated by decode variance + cold prefill; p50-only reports look fine while users churn.

rule.statement: Prefill segment splits cached vs cold; cache_hit_rate is reported. Mixing the two hides the true cost of cold prefill.

rationale.source: Anthropic prompt-caching guide; OpenAI batch + cache stack

rule.rationale: Cached prefill is 90% cheaper; aggregating across hit/miss makes optimisation guesswork.

example.title: Segmented waterfall
example.code: segment p50 p95 budget status ttfb 120ms 180ms 200ms OK prefill_cold 1.1s 1.6s 800ms OVER (cache_hit=0.32 → opportunity) prefill_hit 140ms 220ms 300ms OK decode_tps 85tps 60tps 100tps OVER (tail) tool_search 240ms 410ms 500ms OK post_process 30ms 50ms 100ms OK

example.title: Aggregate only
example.note: No segments; no insight into where to optimise.
